scope: sociology journals and adjacent social-science papers related to dynamic word embeddings / semantic drift

sources.fit: core sociology journal; historical/dynamic word embeddings
sources.title: The Geometry of Culture: Analyzing the Meanings of Class through Word Embeddings
sources.authors: Austin C. Kozlowski, Matt Taddy, James A. Evans
sources.method: word2vec; cultural dimensions; Google Ngrams and decade-sliced JSTOR sociology corpus; survey validation
sources.domain: class, affluence, education, cultivation, status, cultural meaning
sources.evidence: ASR article analyzes millions of books over 100 years; Sage page notes decade windows and JSTOR sociology corpus in appendix/online text.
sources.interpretation: Flagship sociology paper translating DWE into cultural-meaning trajectories; SOUL analogue: stable cultural dimensions with drifting markers.

sources.fit: core sociology-adjacent open journal; diachronic bias/social change
sources.title: Stereotypical Gender Associations in Language Have Decreased Over Time
sources.authors: Jason J. Jones, Mohammad Ruhul Amin, Jessica Kim, Steven Skiena
sources.method: decade-level word embeddings on English-language books, 1800-2000; gender-domain association measures
sources.domain: gender ideology, stereotypes, career/family/science/arts, social change
sources.evidence: Sociological Science/NSF pages list volume 7, pages 1-35, DOI, and word embeddings over millions of digitized books.
sources.interpretation: Direct example of embedding-based social-change hypothesis testing.

sources.fit: sociology/cultural journal; explicit variable/fixed embedding spaces for change
sources.title: Cultural cartography with word embeddings
sources.authors: Dustin S. Stoltz, Marshall A. Taylor
sources.method: fixed embedding space vs variable embedding space; immigration discourse case
sources.domain: cultural sociology, immigration discourse, social marking, media fields, echo chambers, diffusion/change
sources.evidence: ScienceDirect abstract presents fixed/variable embedding methods and U.S. immigration discourse application.
sources.interpretation: Best conceptual bridge from DWE mechanics to sociological theory of meaning.

sources.title: Theoretical Foundations and Limits of Word Embeddings: What Types of Meaning can They Capture?
sources.method: theoretical/methodological assessment of word embeddings, structural linguistics, semiotics, cultural sociology
sources.domain: meaning measurement, structuralism, semiotics, cultural sociology
sources.evidence: Sage page lists SMR 53(4):1753-1793 and abstract on coherence, relationality, static-system assumptions.
sources.interpretation: Quality-control paper for avoiding naive drift interpretation.

sources.fit: cultural sociology journal; historical dynamic class salience
sources.title: Symbols of class: A computational analysis of class distinction-making through etiquette, 1922-2017
sources.authors: Andrea Voyer, Zachary D. Kline, Madison Danton
sources.method: word embeddings over etiquette manuals, 1922-2017; salience of class concepts
sources.domain: class distinction, etiquette, meritocracy, status symbols, cultural closure
sources.evidence: ScienceDirect page lists Poetics 94, 101734 and highlights embeddings for class concepts over 1922-2017.
sources.interpretation: Strong time-axis sociology application for concept salience trajectories.

sources.fit: Poetics/cultural sociology; historical embeddings for intersectionality
sources.title: Leveraging the alignment between machine learning and intersectionality: Using word embeddings to measure intersectional experiences of the nineteenth century U.S. South
sources.method: word embeddings for inductive, cultural, intersectional analysis of historical text
sources.domain: race, gender, slavery, Civil War, intersectionality
sources.evidence: ScienceDirect highlights state word embeddings visualize intersectional experiences of slavery and the Civil War.
sources.interpretation: Historical social-category meaning-space case; useful adjacent DWE application.

sources.fit: sociology methods journal; contextual embeddings for affect/social dynamics
sources.title: Contextual Embeddings in Sociological Research: Expanding the Analysis of Sentiment and Social Dynamics
sources.authors: Moeen Mostafavi, Michael D. Porter, Dawn T. Robinson
sources.method: BERTNN; contextual BERT embeddings to expand affective lexicons
sources.domain: affect control theory, sentiment, social dynamics, new concepts
sources.evidence: Sage page lists DOI and abstract on BERTNN estimating affective meanings using contextual usage.
sources.interpretation: Contextualized semantic position method for new concepts; adjacent to dynamic contextual embeddings.

sources.fit: adjacent political methodology; direct dynamic word-vector time-series method
sources.title: A Timely Intervention: Tracking the Changing Meanings of Political Concepts with Word Vectors
sources.method: four time-sensitive word2vec implementations; small-corpus time series; bootstrap resampling and pretraining
sources.domain: political concepts, equality, newspaper coverage over 161 years
sources.evidence: Cambridge page lists Political Analysis 28(1):87-111 and tests time-sensitive word vectors on 161 years of newspaper coverage.
sources.interpretation: Not sociology journal, but essential for small-corpus temporal semantics like SOUL logs.

sources.fit: adjacent high-impact social science; embedding social stereotypes over historical time
sources.title: Word embeddings quantify 100 years of gender and ethnic stereotypes
sources.authors: Nikhil Garg, Londa Schiebinger, Dan Jurafsky, James Zou
sources.method: historical word embeddings; stereotype associations over 20th century; comparison to census/occupation and social trends
sources.evidence: PNAS DOI and established high-impact diachronic embedding application to stereotypes.
sources.interpretation: Adjacent anchor for comparing textual stereotype drift with social indicators.

sources.fit: psychology/social cognition adjacent; stereotype embeddings across social language corpora
sources.title: Gender Stereotypes in Natural Language: Word Embeddings Show Robust Consistency Across Child and Adult Language Corpora of More Than 65 Million Words
sources.authors: Tessa E. S. Charlesworth, Victor Yang, Thomas C. Mann, Benedek Kurdi, Mahzarin R. Banaji
sources.method: word embeddings over child/adult conversations, books, movies, TV; stereotype quantification
sources.domain: gender stereotypes, collective representations, language corpora
sources.evidence: Sage page abstract states 65+ million words across child/adult and media corpora.
sources.interpretation: Corpus/source comparison benchmark for social meanings.

sources.fit: method resource for social/cultural studies; diachronic embedding dataset
sources.title: DUKweb, diachronic word representations from the UK Web Archive corpus
sources.authors: Adam Tsakalidis, Pierpaolo Basile, Marya Bazzi, Mihai Cucuringu, Barbara McGillivray
sources.method: diachronic word representations from UK Web Archive corpus
sources.domain: social/cultural studies resource, web archive, lexical semantic change
sources.evidence: PubMed abstract says lexical semantic change matters for social/cultural studies and diachronic embeddings are standard resources.
sources.interpretation: Reusable DWE resource for contemporary social semantic change.

sources.fit: computational social science method; concept engagement not time-specific
sources.title: Concept Mover’s Distance: measuring concept engagement via word embeddings in texts
sources.authors: Dustin S. Stoltz, Marshall A. Taylor
sources.method: word mover’s distance plus embeddings to measure concept engagement
sources.domain: cultural sociology, text analysis, concept measurement
sources.evidence: RePEc/Springer entry lists Journal of Computational Social Science 2(2):293-313 and DOI.
sources.interpretation: Can become dynamic when applied to time-sliced documents; candidate for LLM Wiki edge strength.
